role: Pluxee hiring Analytics Engineer in Issy-les-Moulineaux, Île-de-France, France |

evidence.work_arrangement: Hybrid: on site and work from home.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Validate data quality and consistency against the provided business information by testing and auditing data... Ensure compliance with data protection law (PII encryption)
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD is dominated by data quality, testing, auditing, compliance, and governance-heavy language throughout multiple sections, with little speed/shipping framing.
evidence.domain_risk: Ensure compliance with data protection law (PII encryption)
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Pluxee is an employee-benefits/fintech-adjacent B2B2C platform, and the JD explicitly names regulatory compliance (data protection law, PII) as an owned responsibility, indicating high domain risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: Hub Lead Data Engineer; Analytics Engineers; Data Engineers; Business teams
evidence.data_team_maturity: Reporting to the Hub Lead Data Engineer, the Hub Analytics Engineer is a member of the Hub Data Analytics team.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The JD describes an established Hub Data Analytics team with a named lead, existing platform (Azure resources), guidelines, and standards, indicating a mid-to-mature function; mid reflects that it is actively growing across multiple markets rather than a single small, highly specialized mature org.
evidence.jd_authorship: Develop and maintain robust, cost efficient, high-performance data pipelines, end-to-end, mixing several technologies like Azure Data Factory pipelines, Pyspark, Python, Rest API, SQL & NoSQL databases
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The JD names extensive specific technologies, patterns, and processes (Azure Data Factory, PySpark, SCD, Data Vault, DevOps/CI-CD) reflecting genuine hiring-manager knowledge of the day-to-day work, mixed with some generic "why join us" copy.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Actively participate in discussions with business teams to understand their specific data needs and decision-making processes
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Business teams are referenced generically across multiple domains without naming a single dominant commercial, product, or finance function, so mixed applies.
evidence.autonomy_level: Facilitator in the iterative process of developing data flows to ensure that business expectations are clearly understood
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role executes pipelines, models, and dashboards against business-defined requirements and follows guidelines set by the Hub team rather than setting overall strategy.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Collaborate with other Analytics Engineers and Data Engineers and follow the guidelines set by the Hub team, ensuring alignment with our highest standards and best practices
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role joins an established Hub Data Analytics team with existing standards and platform (Azure resources) to extend and maintain, an extend/scale framing rather than building from scratch.
evidence.ai_role: responsible for discovering, modeling, and delivering high-quality, reusable data assets that enable dashboards, self-service analytics, and AI applications across the organization
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Candidate's core responsibility is building data assets that downstream AI applications consume.
evidence.testing_framing: Validate data quality and consistency against the provided business information by testing and auditing data to ensure it meets business rules and expectations for reliability
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and auditing data is an explicit, owned responsibility with action verbs, not just a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Ensure compliance with data protection law (PII encryption)
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Compliance and regulatory (PII/data protection law) framing appears explicitly among core responsibilities, alongside repeated data quality/reliability emphasis.
